seoTitle: How to Create Custom Fields in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to add a custom field in Jira to capture project-specific data that the default fields don't cover.
intro: Jira's default fields cover most common needs, but every team eventually hits something specific to their process that isn't tracked out of the box. This guide walks through creating a custom field and attaching it to the right issues.

steps.heading: Go to custom fields settings
steps.body: Open Jira administration and find the custom fields section under system settings.

steps.heading: Create a new field
steps.body: Choose to add a new custom field and select a field type, such as text, dropdown, or date picker.

steps.heading: Name and configure the field
steps.body: Give it a clear name and description so its purpose is obvious to anyone filling it in.

steps.heading: Associate it with screens
steps.body: Add the new field to the screens used by the issue types and projects where it should appear.

steps.heading: Test it on an issue
steps.body: Open an issue where the field was added and confirm it shows up and saves correctly.

faq.question: What field types can I choose for a custom field?
faq.answer: Jira offers types like text, number, date, dropdown, checkbox, and user picker, among others.

faq.question: Can a custom field be required on issue creation?
faq.answer: Yes, field configuration can mark a custom field as required so issues can't be created without a value.

faq.question: Will a new custom field apply to all projects automatically?
faq.answer: No, it only appears where it's been added to the relevant screens, so it needs to be explicitly associated with each project.

faq.question: Can I reuse a custom field across multiple projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the same custom field can be added to screens in multiple projects rather than creating duplicates.
